seed: softness vs severity; signal_vs_noise; open_gate; spiral_counterclockwise; pulse_marks against radial; bottom_weighted; light_vs_dark; tiny_plus; guilt_forgiveness | abstract variation request 132; target batch count 500
title: Contrasting Echoes
reasoning: The emotional tone of 'softness vs severity' is represented through the contrasting palette of powder pink, warm white, carbon black, steel gray, and sharp crimson. The pattern invokes a sense of tension, with the softness of pink and white occupying the lighter spaces and the severity of black and crimson clashing in dense regions, creating a narrative that reflects the struggle between gentle acceptance and harsh reality. The central collision of colors occurs at the intersection of light and dark, visually echoing the push and pull of forgiveness and guilt. Broken pixels are intentionally placed to disrupt the symmetry, symbolizing the imperfections within emotional states. The overall composition resonates with the themes of radial movement and layered complexity, embodying both a pulse of intensity and a faint whisper of tranquility.

layout.name: argument_grid
layout.description: Two patterns fight for control of the 8x8 canvas.

composition.name: rise_vs_fall
composition.idea: Pixels move upward on one side and downward on the other.

referenceExample.title: Awe at the Edge

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
